message:
style: apply clippy lints and idiomatic Rust improvements

Systematic cleanup across the codebase addressing clippy warnings and
Rust idiom improvements:

- Add #[must_use] to accessor methods on database types
- Replace format! with inline variable captures where applicable
- Use io::Error::other() instead of io::Error::new(ErrorKind::Other, ..)
- Replace .map_or(false, ..) with .is_ok_and()
- Use impl Type<'_> instead of impl<'a> Type<'a> for elided lifetimes
- Replace exact float comparison (== 0.0) with epsilon comparison
  (.abs() < f64::EPSILON) to avoid float_cmp warnings
- Use method references (HashSet::len, Message::topic) instead of
  closures where possible
- Simplify raw string literals from r#"..."# to r"..." and br#"..."#
  to br"..." where no hashes are needed
- Use char literals in .contains() instead of single-char strings
- Remove unnecessary .clone() on Copy types
- Replace assert_eq!(expr, false) with assert!(!expr)
- Use .last().copied() instead of Some(*last().unwrap())
- Reorder drop(queue) after store(0) in bulkhead/rate_limit so the
  atomic write happens while still holding the lock
- Add doc attribute to compile_fail_tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
